name: Monstera obliqua 'Tiwintza Form'
aboutText: The Monstera obliqua 'Tiwintza Form' is a highly sought-after locality expression originating from the southeastern Ecuadorian rainforests. This slender epiphyte is distinguished by its narrow, strap-elliptic leaves and a unique, moderate fenestration pattern featuring elongated holes along the midrib. Thriving in the humid, dappled light of the montane understory, it exhibits a distinct, delicate growth habit with an oblique leaf base. As a specialized locality form, it remains a centerpiece for serious Aroid collectors, prized for its refined foliage structure and botanical significance.

fieldNotes.title: Ecuador's Border Obliqua
fieldNotes.author: Aroid Aaron
fieldNotes.content: A locality form associated with Tiwintza in southeastern Ecuador, near the Peruvian border on the Pastaza River headwaters — an area of genuine botanical significance that has been less systematically collected than the higher-profile Ecuadorian Monstera localities. Ultra rare status but surprisingly accessible price tier for material at this rarity level. This suggests either a specific collecting event that produced clonal material at scale, or some ambiguity in the identification. Worth investigating before acquiring at any price.
